title: Aether — a language in your browser
description: A from-scratch ML-family programming language with Hindley–Milner inference, a kind system with higher-kinded types — genuine Functor/Applicative/Monad classes with superclasses — and THREE BACKENDS that all run the same typed program byte-for-byte: a bytecode stack VM, a JavaScript code generator, and a NATIVE WEBASSEMBLY BACKEND that hand-assembles the program into a real `.wasm` module (a from-scratch binary encoder — no wabt, no binaryen), instantiates it in the browser, and runs it — closures via `call_indirect`, constant-space TAIL CALLS (return_call), structural `match`, and ADTs/records/tuples as tagged cells, with printing/show/comparison delegated to imports that reuse the VM's own code so equivalence holds by construction. Its linear-memory heap is reclaimed by a from-scratch, precise, non-moving TRACING GARBAGE COLLECTOR (mark-sweep, hand-written in wasm) whose root finder is a SHADOW STACK codegen keeps in lock-step with the hidden wasm operand stack — a GC STRESS MODE collects before EVERY allocation and the result stays byte-for-byte identical (proving the root set is complete) while a long allocator loop holds a bounded peak heap instead of growing forever. The WebAssembly backend is also SELF-DESCRIBING: a `name` section lets it DISASSEMBLE ITS OWN BYTES back into readable WAT text via a from-scratch binary decoder (the mirror of the encoder — no wabt, no wasm2wat) that resolves every call/global/local to a `$name`, and a shared SMALL-INTEGER CACHE lets the allocator reuse cells instead of boxing fresh ones (invisible to results since every value is compared structurally), with the module counting the allocations it saved. You can download the `.wasm` and run it anywhere. All three backends compile the output of a real, multi-pass OPTIMIZING MIDDLE-END that rewrites the elaborated core to a fixpoint — constant folding + algebra, β/η-reduction, capture-avoiding inlining, dead-binding elimination, KNOWN-CONSTRUCTOR `match` reduction, record field projection and COMMON-SUBEXPRESSION ELIMINATION — so type-class abstraction MELTS AWAY (a `class` method call on a literal constructor folds all the way to a single literal) and repeated work runs ONCE. CSE is backed by a from-scratch INTERPROCEDURAL EFFECT-&-TOTALITY ANALYSIS that proves which functions are effect-free and total, so even a repeated *call* to a pure helper is shared — and it only ever merges effect-free expressions on a guaranteed-evaluated path, so it can never lose an effect or add a step. The middle-end also compiles pattern matching to GOOD DECISION TREES (Maranget, 2008): instead of re-testing a shared constructor prefix once per arm, it tests each scrutinee position ONCE — a core-to-core lowering into single-column `match`es plus join-points (handling guards and non-exhaustive MATCH_FAIL) that every backend runs unchanged, with a panel that draws the tree. Every rewrite is proven semantics-preserving by the same byte-for-byte equivalence checks, surfaced in an Optimizer panel with the per-rule rewrite breakdown, a round-by-round fixpoint trace, and the VM-step reduction. Plus DERIVING `(Eq, Ord, Show, Enum, Bounded, Functor, Foldable)` synthesised from a data type's shape, property-based testing that generates inputs from inferred types and shrinks failures, monadic do-notation, list comprehensions, an interactive HM derivation tree, a time-travel debugger, and turtle graphics.
